title: Obscure Feedback of Authentication Information
description: Obscure feedback of authentication information during the authentication process to protect the information from possible exploitation/use by unauthorized individuals.

checks.name: Password is Masked During Entry
checks.purpose: Verify the Windows logon screen masks password characters and does not display them in clear text during entry

checks.name: Last Logged-On Username is Not Displayed
checks.purpose: Confirm the Interactive logon: Do not display last user name GPO setting is enabled to prevent username enumeration at the logon screen

checks.name: Detailed Error Messages are Suppressed on Authentication Failure
checks.purpose: Verify authentication failure messages do not reveal whether the username or password was incorrect to prevent user enumeration

checks.name: Last Logged-On Username is Not Displayed
checks.purpose: Confirm the Interactive logon: Do not display last user name GPO setting is enabled on this server

checks.name: Detailed Error Messages are Suppressed on Authentication Failure
checks.purpose: Verify authentication failure messages do not reveal account enumeration information

checks.name: Logon Banner Does Not Reveal System or User Information
checks.purpose: Confirm the pre-logon banner message does not reveal usernames, system names, or configuration details that could assist an attacker

checks.name: SSH Does Not Reveal Whether Username or Password Failed
checks.purpose: Verify sshd is configured to provide a generic authentication failure message and does not distinguish between invalid username and invalid password

checks.name: PAM Does Not Display Verbose Authentication Failure Reasons
checks.purpose: Confirm PAM modules do not output verbose failure messages that reveal account existence or credential specifics

checks.name: SSH Login Banner Does Not Reveal System Details
checks.purpose: Verify the SSH banner configured in sshd_config does not reveal hostname, OS version, or kernel information to unauthenticated users

checks.name: SSH Does Not Reveal Whether Username or Password Failed
checks.purpose: Verify sshd provides a generic authentication failure message on Debian

checks.name: PAM Does Not Display Verbose Authentication Failure Reasons
checks.purpose: Confirm PAM modules do not output verbose failure messages on Debian

checks.name: SSH Login Banner Does Not Reveal System Details
checks.purpose: Verify the SSH banner does not reveal hostname, OS version, or kernel information on Debian

remediation: Enable the Interactive logon: Do not display last user name GPO setting. Ensure authentication failure messages use generic wording. On Linux, configure sshd to use a generic failure message and ensure the SSH banner file does not contain OS or kernel details. Review /etc/issue and /etc/issue.net to remove system-identifying information.
